rows.title: Lunch at Cafe Soleil
rows.notes: Order the Mediterranean Chicken Wrap — satisfying and light enough for an afternoon start.
rows.planning_note: Café Soleil is convenient for a quick, yet delightful lunch just as you arrive and before exploring nearby.

rows.title: Travel to Zion Human History Museum
Zion Human History Museum
rows.notes: Look left of the Virgin River to see the 'Chinaman's Hat' formation.
rows.planning_note: Starting with the museum introduces the park's cultural and geological background, enriching the upcoming outdoor experiences.

rows.title: Dinner at Spotted Dog Cafe
rows.notes: Savour the Utah trout — locally sourced and perfectly cooked.
rows.planning_note: Spotted Dog Cafe offers a cozy setting ideal for unwinding after travel and settling into the Zion experience.

rows.title: Breakfast at Zion Lodge
rows.notes: Enjoy the hearty waffle breakfast — enough energy for a morning of exploration.
rows.planning_note: A solid breakfast gives the necessary energy for an active morning in the canyon.

rows.title: Zion Canyon Shuttle to The Grotto
rows.notes: Board at Zion Visitor Center stop. Free, runs every 6–8 minutes in peak season. No private vehicles beyond this point.

rows.title: Angels Landing Hike
rows.notes: Use your feet on the sandstone; don't rely solely on the chains.
rows.planning_note: Start early to avoid crowds and enjoy cooler temperatures. A highlight of Zion, this strenuous hike offers breathtaking views worth the early rise.

rows.title: Shuttle back to Visitor Center / parking
Lunch at Bit & Spur Restaurant
rows.notes: Try the Southwestern Tacos — a local favorite.
rows.planning_note: A refreshing pause with local flavors after the morning hike.

rows.title: Weeping Rock Geological Marvel
rows.notes: Stand at the innermost curve for the finest mist experience from the spring-fed sandstone.
rows.planning_note: Ideal for easy afternoon exploration — is accessible and contrasts the strenuous morning with a peaceful atmosphere.

rows.title: Dinner at King's Landing Bistro
rows.notes: Enjoy the bison meatloaf — renowned for its rich flavor.
rows.planning_note: Dining options reflect the local spirit, capping a day of Zion's highlights with a savory meal.

rows.title: Breakfast at Oscar's Cafe
rows.notes: Opt for the Breakfast Burrito — it's hearty enough to fuel your day.
rows.planning_note: A well-rounded breakfast to prepare for a day exploring Zion's natural wonders.

rows.title: Riverside Walk Trail
rows.notes: Ideal spots to safely access the Virgin River are about halfway along.
rows.planning_note: A gentle introduction to Zion’s water features, offering scenic, hassle-free walking as the day begins.

rows.title: The Narrows Hike
rows.notes: Rent canyoneering shoes and a walking stick for the best experience.
rows.planning_note: A unique Zion hike, this is an immersive experience into the park’s iconic river-worn canyons.

rows.title: Shuttle back to Visitor Center / parking
Lunch at Cafe Soleil
rows.notes: Try the Vegan Portabella Sandwich for a refreshing vegetarian choice.
rows.planning_note: Rejuvenate with a quality meal in familiar settings following active river exploration.

rows.title: Canyon Overlook Trail
rows.notes: Pause at the alcove for a unique frame of Pine Creek Canyon and the Virgin River.
rows.planning_note: An iconic short hike providing a high-impact view, ideal for an afternoon when the light enhances the landscape.

rows.title: Dinner at Switchback Grille
rows.notes: Indulge in the Prime Rib — a generous portion that's locally renowned.
rows.planning_note: Conclude the day with a hearty meal — a local favorite that celebrates the region’s robust flavors.

rows.title: Breakfast at Deep Creek Coffee
rows.notes: Fuel up with the hearty Avocado Toast, packed with protein and flavor.
rows.planning_note: Start with a vibrant breakfast option to kick off a scenic day in Kolob.

rows.title: Travel to Kolob Canyons Scenic Drive
rows.notes: Drive to the western section of the park, Kolob Canyons, known for its less crowded, dramatic scenic beauty.

rows.title: Kolob Canyons Scenic Drive
rows.notes: Stop at Timber Creek Overlook for views not confined to the main park area.
rows.planning_note: Early start ensures clear views and a serene atmosphere, spotlighting the region's solitude.

rows.title: Timber Creek Overlook Trail
rows.notes: For optimal observation, watch how shadows emphasize the sandstone patterns.
rows.planning_note: A short trail that provides a grand sense of place and remains often overlooked.

rows.title: Return to Springdale
rows.notes: Return through the scenic landscape back to the comforts of Springdale.

rows.title: Lunch at Zion Pizza & Noodle Co.
rows.notes: The Mountain Man Pizza is perfect for adventurous food lovers.
rows.planning_note: A fulfilling meal to recharge after a morning spent in Kolob's remote beauty.

rows.title: Observation Point via East Mesa
rows.notes: Spot the late-autumn reds and golds from the scrub oak below the viewpoint.
rows.planning_note: This trail offers high reward views without the usual crowds, perfect for a day's culmination in Zion’s landscapes.

rows.title: Dinner at Springdale BBQ Company
rows.notes: Indulge in the mixed BBQ platter for an authentic taste of local cuisine.
rows.planning_note: Wrap up the day with bold flavors typical of the region, offering a satisfying contrast to the day's visual feast.

rows.title: Breakfast at Wildcat Willies Ranch Grill & Saloon
rows.notes: Enjoy the Ranchero Omelette — packed with local flavors to start your travel day.
rows.planning_note: A flavorful start ensuring energy for departure activities and travel.

rows.title: Travel from Springdale to Las Vegas Airport
rows.notes: Depart Zion via UT-9 W and I-15 S for your journey home.

change_log: No changes — itinerary passed all checks.
